stories.headline: Will Elon Musk appear before the French courts?
stories.summary: The billionaire is being summoned on Monday by the Paris public prosecutor's office as part of an investigation into his social network X and its AI tool Grok, which are suspected of algorithmic manipulation to interfere in the French political debate.

stories.headline: Colombia Kicks Off Global Bond Buyback Ahead of Elections
stories.summary: Colombia is buying back some of its outstanding global bonds, its third such operation in the past year, as part of a broader strategy to reduce borrowing costs weeks ahead of a tight presidential race.

stories.headline: EQB Inc. Looks to Sell C$200M Limited Recourse Capital Notes
stories.summary: Canada’s EQB Inc., a bank holding company, is preparing to sell around C$200 million ($146 million) of additional tier one securities as soon as Monday, according to people with knowledge of the matter.

stories.headline: Strategy Buys $2.54 Billion of Bitcoin, Most Since Late 2024
stories.summary: Michael Saylor’s Strategy Inc. bought $2.54 billion in Bitcoin over the previous seven days, marking the digital asset treasury company’s largest acquisition of the original cryptocurrency since November 2024.

stories.headline: Psychedelic Stocks Soar as Trump Signs Order to Ease Access
stories.summary: Shares of companies involved in psychedelic-tied therapies are surging in Monday trading after President Donald Trump signed an executive order to expedite research and access.

stories.headline: USA Rare Earth to Buy Brazilian Miner in $2.8 Billion Deal

stories.headline: Honeywell to Sell Productivity Business in $1.4 Billion Deal
stories.summary: Honeywell International Inc. agreed to divest its productivity solutions and services business to industrial manufacturer Brady Corp., the latest move by Honeywell to reshape its portfolio as it pursues a multipart breakup.

stories.headline: Citadel Securities Pitches Lower Tick-Size Pilot Program to SEC
stories.summary: Citadel Securities asked the Securities and Exchange Commission to test reducing the price increments that some stocks and ETFs trade at before embarking on major market structure changes.

stories.headline: Banks Rejected By Supreme Court in Muni Bond Price-Fixing Case
stories.summary: The US Supreme Court refused to block a class action lawsuit that seeks billions of dollars from Bank of America Corp., JPMorgan Chase & Co. and other financial institutions for allegedly fixing prices in the municipal bond market.

stories.headline: AI Junk-Debt Offering Wave Rolls On as Edged Compute Sells Bonds

stories.headline: Kuwait Declares Force Majeure on Oil Shipments on Hormuz Halt

stories.headline: Eni, Repsol Reach Deal to Start Exporting Venezuelan Gas in 2031
stories.summary: Eni SpA and Repsol SA plan to start exporting natural gas from Venezuela by the end of 2031 after reaching a deal with Caracas to revive a long-stalled effort to expand production from a massive offshore field.

stories.headline: Big Names Wait in the Wings as Virginians Decide Their House Maps
stories.summary: With Virginians voting Tuesday to accept or reject redistricting, candidates from both parties await the voters’ judgment to decide whether — or where — to run for Congress.
